name: Lawrence Sutin
key_works: In Pursuit of VALIS: Selections from the Exegesis (OceanofPDF edition)
Divine Invasions: A Life of Philip K. Dick
In Pursuit of VALIS: Selections from the Exegesis
The Shifting Realities of Philip K. Dick: Selected Literary and Philosophical Writings

archive_pdfs.title: Divine Invasions: A Life of Philip K. Dick
archive_pdfs.filename: Divine Invasions_ A Life of Phi - Lawrence Sutin.pdf

archive_pdfs.title: In Pursuit of VALIS: Selections from the Exegesis
In Pursuit of VALIS: Selections from the Exegesis (OceanofPDF edition)
The Shifting Realities of Philip K. Dick: Selected Literary and Philosophical Writings

interpretive_stance: Author of Divine Invasions (1989), the first major PKD biography. Sutin treats Dick's visionary experiences with sympathetic seriousness while maintaining biographical objectivity. His work established the biographical framework that all subsequent PKD scholarship builds on.
relevance: Primary source for biography timeline events, dates, and context. Key reference for corroborating or contradicting PKD's self-reports in the Exegesis.

name: Kyle Arnold
key_works: The Divine Madness of Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: The Divine Madness of Philip K. Dick
The Divine Madness of Philip K. Dick
The Divine Madness of Philip K. Dick
archive_pdfs.filename: Arnold Kyle_Dick Philip K The divine madness of Philip K Dick Oxford University Press.pdf

interpretive_stance: Author of The Divine Madness of Philip K. Dick (2016). A clinical psychologist, Arnold reads Dick's visionary experiences through the lens of psychobiography, connecting the Exegesis to patterns of temporal lobe phenomena and bipolar disorder while respecting the intellectual content.
relevance: Provides clinical framework for understanding Dick's self-reports. Key source for evaluating reliability of biography events and vision accounts.

name: Anne R. Dick
key_works: The Search for Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: The Search for Philip K. Dick

interpretive_stance: PKD's third wife (1959-1965). Author of The Search for Philip K. Dick (1995), a memoir providing intimate domestic detail about Dick's working methods, drug use, and psychological states during a crucial creative period.
relevance: Primary source for biographical events 1959-1965. Provides a perspective on Dick unavailable from any other source — the daily reality behind the fiction.

name: Anthony Peake
key_works: A Life of Philip K. Dick: The Man Who Remembered the Future

archive_pdfs.title: A Life of Philip K. Dick: The Man Who Remembered the Future

interpretive_stance: Author of A Life of Philip K. Dick: The Man Who Remembered the Future (2013). Peake approaches Dick's visionary experiences through the lens of neuroscience and consciousness studies, proposing that Dick may have accessed genuine anomalous cognition.
relevance: Offers a consciousness-studies framework for interpreting the 2-3-74 experience that complements Sutin's biographical and Arnold's psychological approaches.

name: Tessa B. Dick
key_works: The Owl in Daylight

archive_pdfs.title: The Owl in Daylight

interpretive_stance: PKD's fifth and final wife (1973-1977), present during the 2-3-74 experience. Author of Owl In Daylight and other memoirs. Provides first-person witness testimony of Dick's visionary period.
relevance: The only witness to the 2-3-74 experience itself. Her accounts are essential for corroborating or contextualizing Dick's Exegesis narratives.

name: Gregg Rickman
key_works: Dick, Deception, and Dissociation: A Comment on 'The Two Faces of Philip K. Dick'
Philip K. Dick: In His Own Words
The Nature of Dick's Fantasies (Review)
On Our Philip K. Dick Collection (with Response)

interpretive_stance: Author of multiple PKD interview collections and biographical studies. Rickman conducted extensive interviews with Dick and his associates, producing primary source material that complements the Exegesis.
relevance: Interview transcripts provide Dick's spoken self-commentary, which often differs from his written Exegesis formulations — valuable for tracking contradictions.

name: Andrew M. Butler
key_works: Philip K. Dick
Philip K. Dick (Pocket Essentials, Revised and Updated)
Ontology and Ethics in the Writings of Philip K. Dick (PhD Thesis)

archive_pdfs.title: Philip K. Dick
Philip K. Dick (Pocket Essentials, Revised and Updated)
archive_pdfs.filename: Andrew M Butler Philip K Dick _ Revised and Updated Oldcastle Books.pdf

archive_pdfs.title: Ontology and Ethics in the Writings of Philip K. Dick (PhD Thesis)

interpretive_stance: PhD thesis on PKD. Author of critical guides to Dick's work. Butler applies literary and cultural theory to Dick's fiction, situating it within broader SF criticism and postmodern thought.
relevance: Provides academic critical framework for understanding Dick's fiction in relation to the Exegesis's self-commentary on his novels.

name: Carl Freedman
key_works: Towards a Theory of Paranoia: The Science Fiction of Philip K. Dick
Editorial Introduction: Philip K. Dick and Criticism

interpretive_stance: Marxist SF critic who reads Dick through the lens of critical theory and political economy. Emphasizes the social and political dimensions of Dick's fiction.
relevance: Offers political readings that complement the theological focus of the Exegesis, relevant to entries on Empire, Rome, and Nixon.

name: James Burton
key_works: Machines Making Gods: Philip K. Dick, Henri Bergson and Saint Paul
The Philosophy of Science Fiction: Henri Bergson and the Fabulations of Philip K. Dick

interpretive_stance: PKD scholar with multiple publications in the archive.

name: Jeffrey J. Kripal
key_works: Authors of the Impossible: The Paranormal and the Sacred
Mutants & Mystics: Science Fiction, Superhero Comics, and the Paranormal

archive_pdfs.title: Authors of the Impossible: The Paranormal and the Sacred
archive_pdfs.filename: Kripal, Jeffrey J - Authors of the impossible _ the paranormal and the sacred (2011,2010, The University of Chicago Press) - libgen.li.pdf

archive_pdfs.title: Mutants & Mystics: Science Fiction, Superhero Comics, and the Paranormal
archive_pdfs.filename: Kripal, Jeffrey J - Mutants & mystics_ science fiction, superhero comics, and the paranormal (2011_2015, University of Chicago Press) - libgen.li.pdf

interpretive_stance: Author of works on the paranormal in American religion. Kripal treats Dick's visionary experiences as part of a broader pattern of anomalous religious experience in American culture, neither dismissing nor endorsing their ontological claims.
relevance: Provides a religious studies framework for understanding Dick's experiences that takes them seriously as religious phenomena without requiring clinical or theological commitment.

name: Umberto Rossi
key_works: Errata: L'oscuro scrutare di Philip K. Dick (Review)
The Holy Family from Outer Space: Reconsidering Philip K. Dick's The Divine Invasion

archive_pdfs.title: The Holy Family from Outer Space: Reconsidering Philip K. Dick's The Divine Invasion
archive_pdfs.filename: [Extrapolation 2011-jan vol. 52 iss. 2] The Holy Family from Outer Space_ Reconsidering Philip K. Dick&_039_s The Divine Inva...{Rossi, Umberto}(2011 January)[10.3828_extr.2011.52.2.2]{66776860} libgen.li.pdf

archive_pdfs.title: Errata: L'oscuro scrutare di Philip K. Dick (Review)
archive_pdfs.filename: [Science Fiction Studies 2008-mar vol. 35 iss. 1] Errata_ L&_039_oscuro scutare di Philip K. Dick (Philip K. Dick&_039_s Dark Scanning){Review by_ Umberto Rossi}(2008 March)[10.2307_25475127]{33275697} libgen.li.pdf

interpretive_stance: Italian PKD scholar publishing in English. Brings European literary-critical perspectives to Dick's work, often emphasizing intertextual and semiotic dimensions.
relevance: Provides non-Anglophone scholarly perspectives on Dick, useful for international context in dictionary entries.

name: Angus Taylor
key_works: Philip K. Dick and the Umbrella of Light

archive_pdfs.title: Philip K. Dick and the Umbrella of Light

interpretive_stance: Personal friend of Dick who confirmed Dick's philosophy was 'through-going Platonistic.' Taylor brought philosophical rigor to conversations with Dick that influenced the Exegesis.
relevance: Direct personal testimony about Dick's philosophical self-understanding. His confirmation of Dick's Platonism is cited in our Plato entry.

name: Darko Suvin
key_works: Philip K. Dick's Opus: Artifice as Refuge and World View (1975)

archive_pdfs.title: Philip K. Dick's Opus: Artifice as Refuge and World View (1975)

interpretive_stance: Foundational science fiction theorist. His concept of 'cognitive estrangement' as the defining feature of SF deeply influences how Dick's work is read academically.
relevance: Provides the theoretical framework within which Dick's fiction is most commonly analyzed in academic SF studies.

name: David F. Dufty
key_works: How to Build an Android: The True Story of Philip K. Dick's Robotic Resurrection

archive_pdfs.title: How to Build an Android: The True Story of Philip K. Dick's Robotic Resurrection

interpretive_stance: Author of How to Build an Android (2012), documenting the creation of the Philip K. Dick android. Approaches Dick through the lens of AI and robotics history.
relevance: Provides context for Dick's legacy in AI and technology, relevant to terms like 'android,' 'simulacra,' and the fiction-reality boundary.

name: David Sandner
key_works: Philip K. Dick: Essays of the Here and Now

archive_pdfs.title: Philip K. Dick: Essays of the Here and Now
Philip K. Dick: Essays of the Here and Now
archive_pdfs.filename: David Sandner Donald E Palumbo C W Sullivan III Philip K Dick_ Essays of the Here and Now McFarland.pdf

interpretive_stance: Editor of Philip K. Dick: Essays of the Here and Now. Sandner collects and frames contemporary critical approaches to Dick's work.
relevance: Provides a cross-section of current PKD criticism useful for identifying scholarly debates our dictionary entries should engage with.

name: Douglas Kellner
key_works: The Apocalyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: The Apocalyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick
archive_pdfs.filename: PDF The Apocolyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick.pdf

interpretive_stance: Co-author (with Steven Best) of 'The Apocalyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick.' Critical theorist who reads Dick through Frankfurt School and cultural studies lenses.
relevance: Relevant to dictionary entries on apocalypse, empire, and Dick's political theology.

name: Emmanuel Levinas
key_works: The Name of a Dog, or Natural Rights — Emmanuel Levinas

archive_pdfs.title: The Name of a Dog, or Natural Rights — Emmanuel Levinas

interpretive_stance: Major 20th-century philosopher. His work 'Name of the Dog' in the archive provides philosophical context for Dick's ethical and theological concerns.
relevance: Philosophical source relevant to Dick's ethics of otherness and responsibility.

name: Gabriel McKee
key_works: "I Am a Machine": Programming and Control in Dick's Exegesis (PKD Festival 2024)

archive_pdfs.title: "I Am a Machine": Programming and Control in Dick's Exegesis (PKD Festival 2024)

interpretive_stance: Presented 'I Am a Machine: Programming and Control in Dick's Exegesis' at PKD Festival 2024. Focuses on the cybernetic and information-theoretic dimensions of Dick's theology.
relevance: Recent scholarship engaging directly with the Exegesis text, relevant to our dictionary entries on information, programming, and determinism.

name: Lorenzo DiTommaso
key_works: Gnosticism and Dualism in the Early Fiction of Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: Gnosticism and Dualism in the Early Fiction of Philip K. Dick
archive_pdfs.filename: [Science Fiction Studies 2001-mar vol. 28 iss. 1] Gnosticism and Dualism in the Early Fiction of Philip K. Dick{Lorenzo DiTommaso}(2001 March)[10.2307_4240950]{33300601} libgen.li.pdf

interpretive_stance: Scholar of apocalypticism and religious studies who examines Dick's work within the history of apocalyptic thought.
relevance: Directly relevant to Exegesis entries on Parousia, Kingdom, eschatology, and Dick's prophetic self-understanding.

name: Norman Spinrad
key_works: Science Fiction in the Real World

archive_pdfs.title: Science Fiction in the Real World

interpretive_stance: SF author and critic who wrote about Dick in Science Fiction in the Real World. Spinrad approaches Dick as a peer within the SF community, offering practitioner-level literary analysis.
relevance: Provides SF-community insider perspective on Dick's literary reputation and working methods.

name: Stanislaw Lem
key_works: Statement by Stanislaw Lem Regarding Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: Statement by Stanislaw Lem Regarding Philip K. Dick

interpretive_stance: Polish SF author and theorist who wrote influential criticism of Dick's work, praising his philosophical depth while critiquing his literary execution. One of the few international literary figures to take Dick seriously during his lifetime.
relevance: Lem's criticism represents the most rigorous contemporary evaluation of Dick's philosophical fiction. PKD was aware of and responded to Lem's readings.

name: Steven Best
key_works: The Apocalyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: The Apocalyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick
archive_pdfs.filename: PDF The Apocolyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick.pdf

interpretive_stance: Co-author (with Douglas Kellner) of 'The Apocalyptic Vision of Philip K. Dick,' applying critical theory to Dick's apocalyptic themes.
relevance: Relevant to dictionary entries on apocalypse, empire, and Dick's political theology.

name: Bruce Gillespie
key_works: Scratch Pad 44: The Philip K. Dick Special
SF Commentary, Issue 4
SF Commentary, Issue 31 (Philip Dick in Vancouver)
SF Commentary, Issue 1
SF Commentary, Issue 17

archive_pdfs.title: Scratch Pad 44: The Philip K. Dick Special
SF Commentary, Issue 1
SF Commentary, Issue 17
SF Commentary, Issue 31 (Philip Dick in Vancouver)
SF Commentary, Issue 4

interpretive_stance: Editor of the influential Australian fanzine Science Fiction Commentary, which published early serious critical engagement with Dick's work during his lifetime.
relevance: Historical primary source for how Dick's work was received and discussed in the SF community during his lifetime.

name: Jonathan Lethem
key_works: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
archive_pdfs.filename: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick - Dick_ Philip K_.pdf

interpretive_stance: Co-editor of the published Exegesis (2011) and acclaimed novelist. Lethem approaches Dick as both a literary peer and a cultural figure, emphasizing the aesthetic and literary dimensions of the Exegesis alongside its theological content.
relevance: His editorial introduction and annotations are key secondary sources. His reading of Zebra as Dick's most original theological contribution informs our dictionary entries.

name: Pamela Jackson
key_works: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick

archive_pdfs.title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
archive_pdfs.filename: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick - Dick_ Philip K_.pdf

interpretive_stance: Co-editor (with Jonathan Lethem) of the published Exegesis (2011). Jackson's editorial work shaped which portions of the 8,000-page manuscript reached the public, making her selections a de facto interpretive framework for all subsequent Exegesis scholarship.
relevance: Her editorial decisions about inclusion, ordering, and annotation directly affect how our project relates to the published Exegesis edition.

The Phil Dick-manack, Issue 33,132 (September 2019)

The Phil Dick-manack, Issue 33,132 (September 2019)

relevance: Chronological data useful for dating Exegesis entries and cross-referencing biographical events.
